headline: Circle Internet Shares Slip Following Report of New Stablecoin Initiative by Payments Giants (CRCL)
summary: Circle Internet (NYSE:CRCL) shares fell 2% in Wednesday morning trading after a report suggested that several major payment companies are preparing to launch a competing stablecoin platform. The development raised concerns about intensifying competition in the rapidly expanding digital payments and stablecoin markets.

content.title: Circle Internet Shares Slip Following Report of New Stablecoin Initiative by Payments Giants (CRCL)

The development raised concerns about intensifying competition in the rapidly expanding digital payments and stablecoin markets.

headline: Mastercard goes 24/7 on-chain as bitcoin searches for a bottom
summary: Scott Melker discusses bitcoin (BTC-USD) bottoming, while Mastercard (MA) expands its settlement capabilities to support on-chain, real-time payments that operate 24/7 on blockchain networks. "The Daily Wolf with Scott Melker" airs every day at 12:00 p.m. Tune in for your daily dose of all things crypto. Make sure to also check out Yahoo Finance's new crypto hub to find the latest crypto-related news.

headline: Stripe, Visa, Mastercard close to new stablecoin platform
summary: Stripe, Visa and Mastercard are close to launching a new stablecoin platform, with Coinbase considering joining. The total stablecoin market sits around $320 billion, dominated by Tether's USDT at $187 billion. CoinDesk's Uyen Truong hosts "CoinDesk Daily."

headline: Mastercard Leadership Shakeup Puts Global Growth And Services In Focus
summary: Mastercard (NYSE:MA) has announced a major leadership transition effective August 3, 2026. Ling Hai, currently regional president overseeing Asia Pacific, Europe, Middle East and Africa, will become the next Chief Financial Officer. Current CFO Sachin Mehra will move into a new Chief Business Officer role as part of a wider reshuffle of senior executives. Mastercard operates a global payments network that connects consumers, financial institutions, merchants and governments. Leadership...

headline: Mastercard taps Solana as it brings stablecoin settlement to its global card network
summary: Mastercard is adding regulated stablecoins to the way its card network settles money, and Solana is one of the chains it chose to run them on. In a June 3 announcement, Mastercard said it is expanding its settlement options to add intraday, weekend, and holiday card settlement, alongside on-chain ...
